title: Geometry: Area and Volume
blurb: Placeholder — find area of polygons and surface area and volume of 3D figures.

lessons.title: Area and Volume
lessons.concreteComparison: Placeholder — real-world opener authored in Step 3. Example: painting a wall means finding its area; filling a fish tank means finding its volume.
lessons.objective: Placeholder — calculate the area of rectangles, triangles, and parallelograms; find volume of rectangular prisms.
concept: Placeholder concept paragraph. Area measures the space inside a 2D shape. Volume measures the space inside a 3D solid.

workedExamples.statement: Placeholder problem. Find the area of a triangle with base 8 cm and height 5 cm.

tryIt.statement: Placeholder try-it. Find the volume of a box that is 4 m long, 3 m wide, and 2 m tall.

practice.statement: Placeholder practice problem. Find the area of a parallelogram with base 10 cm and height 6 cm.

testYourself.statement: Placeholder test problem. A rectangular prism has length 5, width 4, height 3. Find surface area.
